programmes.show_description: Jimmy Doherty and Shivi Ramoutar are joined in the kitchen by actor and comedian Kevin Bishop, who enjoys black pudding wontons for breakfast and a sweet spicy treat of a pineapple and chilli ccrème brûlée. Chantal Dawson hast tips on how to save ailing houseplants, Shivi learns how to make a natural woodland fire torch with bushcraft and survival expert Mark Beckham, and Jimmy gets up close and personal with some crocodiles and alligators.
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

The competition is heating up with just 49 players left as Rob Brydon hosts another tense round of quick-fire quiz duels, with each win expanding a player's territory, while each loss sends another home. With a £5,000 Floor bonus on the line and a £50,000 jackpot up for grabs at the end of the series, it's all to play for as friends turn to foes and challenge one another
Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features
Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more
The key headlines from the day so far

Stephen Mulhern hosts another charity edition of the game show, with Gyles Brandreth, Cliff Parisi and Perri Shakes-Drayton trying to say what they see as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

Adil Ray hosts the game in which three pairs of contestants use their skills to find words from the fewest letters possible. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to potentially double the money they won earlier or leave with nothing. Taking part alongside the reigning champions are sisters Harriet and Ella, and best friends Steve and Dave
Stephen Mulhern hosts as one of the brave contestants takes on the infamous Banker and opens those iconic 22 red boxes with the chance of winning a life-changing cash prize.

Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot
Bradley Walsh hosts as Anthony from London, Katy from Tunbridge Wells, Nicholas from Burnley and Cathy from Swansea form a brave team willing to take on a Chaser and hopefully earn a cash prize

programmes.show_name: STV News at Six and Weather
programmes.show_description: Including regional weather forecast
A round-up of the day's news headlines and talking points from the UK and around the world
Movie news, chat about favourite TV programmes, and music. Plus, catch-ups with some of the biggest names in worldwide entertainment
Isaac is upset that his dad has cancer, while Cain is preoccupied in his search for farmland and Pollard tests Kerry's loyalty
Daniel seeks answers from Megan about the future of their relationship, and Theo hatches a new plan, announcing that he's been offered a job in Belfast before spiking an oblivious Todd's drink

Noah Solloway escapes the city for a break in Long Island with his wife and four children, where a chance encounter leads him to begin an affair with waitress Alison Bailey. Golden Globe-winning American drama, starring Dominic West and Ruth Wilson
A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programmes.show_name: Devil in Disguise: John Wayne Gacy
programmes.show_description: Kunkle and Sam are knee-deep in Gacy's trial as Tovar volunteers to drive Gacy to and from court, while attempting to compel him to come clean about how many people he killed, the disturbing tale of how Gacy befriended and groomed David Cram is revealed

Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz where each episode sees six hopefuls compete for £1 million - however, only one player can take their place in the Hot Seat at a time and answer multiple-choice questions to work their way up the Money Ladder

The final four make the last terrifying ascent, with obstacles in the way including a dangerous crevasse crossing that sees one of them frozen in fear and another's attempt made harder by the menacing Mountain's Keeper still lurking around, Who will make it to the Summit and claim their share of the cash prize?
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

Life-changing money is on the line as Ant and Dec welcome two new pairs to the Limitless Ladder. First there is Tegan and Paul, who are playing for an early retirement for Paul, a new house for Tegan and treats for the whole family, but can they navigate the ups and downs of the ladder without crashing out? Football team-mates and kindred spirits Abbi and Amy then take their shot, with Amy having her heart set on travelling the world
Adil Ray hosts the game of words as Emmerdale actors Lisa Riley and Mark Charnock take on the Rev Richard Coles and comedian Tim Vine. They all try to win big for their chosen charities

Alan Titchmarsh and his team create a cross between a woodland glade and a forest school for care worker Carl and his family, and transform a back garden into a magical slice of nature in Macclesfield. Meanwhile, Frances Tophill has the challenge of bringing the feeling of a woodland to a tiny, concrete courtyard in Accrington

programmes.show_name: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programmes.show_description: Joining Alan at Manor Farm are actor James Murray and writer Lynda La Plante, while florist Jonathan Moseley has innovative ideas for Mothering Sunday, and Andy Clarke serves up his Mother's Day martinis in the Best of British feature
James welcomes singer Alfie Boe and Loose Women presenter Penny Lancaster to his Hampshire home, while also sampling recipes from fellow chefs Paul Rankin and Shivi Ramoutar

Ipswich Town v Millwall (Kick-off 12.30pm). Hugh Woozencroft presents coverage of the Championship match at Portman Road, as the promotion contenders meet for the second time this season. The reverse fixture at The Den on Boxing Day finished in a 0-0 draw, and while a point today wouldn't been seen as a disaster for either of the play-off hopefuls, both will be eager to claim all three points as they continue to push for a place in the top two. With analysis from Darren Bent and Tony Cascarino, and commentary by Sam Matterface and Iain Dowie
Round-up of the latest headlines

Ben Shephard asks the questions as Ollie Locke, Denise Van Outen and Axel Blake answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

Bradley Walsh presents as Cherry Healey, Dallas Campbell, Ria Hebden and Robert Peston pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught
Round-up of the latest headlines
Joe Swash, Joanna Page, and Angellica Bell join Stephen Mulhern as they try to 'say what they see' and guess well-known phrases for their chance to win 50,000 pounds for charity

The auditions make their way to Birmingham's Hippodrome Theatre, where guest judge Stacey Solomon - who knows a thing or two about auditioning in front of a live audience, after coming third on The X Factor in 2009 - joins the panel alongside KSI, Alesha Dixon and Amanda Holden

Prank show in which Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry work together to deceive unsuspecting members of the public by making them believe they are participating in new ITV shows. The first set of unsuspecting 'contestants' think they are taking part in business competition The Applicant, hosted by Dragons' Den star Sara Davies - who is in on the joke - while Jo Brand joins in the fun from Mission HQ as the rebellious guest prankster

Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?

Jonathan chats to Gavin and Stacey co-creator Ruth Jones, Ready or Not 2: Here I Come co-stars Sarah Michelle Gellar and Kathryn Newton, comedian, writer and director Richard Ayoade and Great British Bake Off presenter Alison Hammond. With music from sibling duo the Molotovs
